title: Sam Schrager's questions for Brink, conducting interview from her home.
description: Sam Schrager's questions for Carol Brink.

title: Carol's childhood in a small town, lonely but happy; why Idaho as source of material
description: Beginning of Carol Brink's reply. Sources of writing in her Idaho childhood.

title: Her book Buffalo Coat; real facts verses fiction; double suicide story; Moscow
description: History of doctors in Buffalo Coat: Dr. Dellapine. Dr. Ledbrook and Winnie Booth suicides were a love pact, as the book suggests. Mrs. Ledbrook. First draft of Buffalo Coat: need to make a good story from facts.

title: Biographical information about her grandfather, Dr. W. W. Watkins
description: Personal history of Dr. W. W. Watkins, her grandfather, his life as a doctor delivering babies, and his role in Moscow.

title: Biographical information about her Grandmother Watkins
Grandmother Watkins; children's books
description: Grandmother Watkins (Caddie Woodlawn), and her home in Moscow, where Mrs. Brink grew up. Moscow friends featured in her children's books, Two are Better Than One, All Over Town, and Luley.

title: Social classes, diversity in Moscow
description: Diversity of backgrounds in people who came to Moscow. Definite social classes in Moscow.

title: Women's role in pioneer society, West verses East
description: Equality of women in pioneer West as compared to East. They believed that their role was as important as men's. Significance of Caddie Woodlawn's father speaking to her about growing up; this led some women's libbers (women's liberation) to condemn book.

title: Grandmother's home in Wisconsin
description: Her grandmother's home near Menominee, Wisconsin, has been preserved by the Dunn County Historical Society as part of the Caddie Woodlawn Park.

title: Biographical information about her father, Alexander Ryrie; her family
description: Background of father, Alexander Ryrie, and his brothers, the basis for Snow in the River. The fun of pioneering. Mother and aunt.

title: Girl Scouts troupe; Sunday School
description: Play of the Sunday school girls group.

title: Homesteading near Clarkia (Washington)
description: Homesteading east of Clarkia. Conflict between lumber company and conservationists. Effect of homesteading on her aunt: her struggle one winter. Accuracy of Strangers in the Forest.

title: Boville family; girls gaining weight; lumberjack named Dynamite
description: Life for girls visiting on the homestead. The Bovills. Wages for the girls to gain weight. How Dynamite got his name.

title: Husband was supportive of her work as a female writer
description: Friendship and support of her husband. Writing as a woman.

title: Sketches of early Moscow
description: Mrs. Brink reads sketches of early Moscow. "Proudly We Hailed": Moscow Fourth of July. This was deleted from the transcription.

title: Sketches of Unimportant People
description: Unpublished true stories from Unimportant People. "Of Tramps and My Aunt." This was deleted from the transcription.

title: Sketches of The Bookstore
description: "The Bookstore." This was deleted from the transcription.

title: Perspective with old age; awareness of a small town
description: Perspective of old age. Awareness that comes from growing up in a small town. Greetings to Moscow.
